question.content: For a bridge-crane girder AB with span \(l=16\,\text{m}\), the original maximum lifting load is \(90\,\text{kN}\). Use the rigging configuration given in the accompanying image: a rope is hung at point C located \(x\) from end B, passes around a pulley on the lifted object, and the other end is attached to the crane hook; the crane then moves to point D, symmetric to C. In this way, a \(150\,\text{kN}\) load can be lifted. Determine the maximum \(x\), considering only normal-stress strength of the girder. Answer format: a single numerical value; do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Set two load cases for the same girder section modulus \(W\). Case 1 (original design): a simply supported beam with midspan concentrated load \(F=90\,\text{kN}\), so \(M_1=\frac{Fl}{4}=\frac{90\times16}{4}=360\,\text{kN}\cdot\text{m}\). Case 2 (modified rigging): two equal loads of \(75\,\text{kN}\), and the critical section is at C/D with \(M_2=75x\,\text{kN}\cdot\text{m}\).

step_3.content: Because the original \(90\,\text{kN}\) design already reaches allowable normal stress at the critical point, require \(\sigma_2\le\sigma_1\): \(\frac{M_2}{W}\le\frac{M_1}{W}\Rightarrow\frac{75x}{W}\le\frac{360}{W}\Rightarrow 75x\le360\).

step_11.content: Solve the inequality: \(x\le\frac{360}{75}=4.8\,\text{m}\). Hence the maximum allowable distance is \(x_{\max}=4.8\,\text{m}\).
